title: Working with Strings
description: Delve deeper into text manipulation with strings.

slides.title: Strings 🧵
slides.content:
The `string` type is used to store a sequence of characters (text).

Unlike `char`, a string value must be surrounded by **double quotes** `""`.

slides.title: The String Library 📚
slides.content: To use strings, you must include an additional header file in the source code, the `<string>` library.

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: What header must be included to use the string data type?
quizData.explanation: You must #include <string> to use C++ strings.

slides.title: String Concatenation 🔗
slides.content: The `+` operator can be used between strings to add them together to make a new string. This is called **concatenation**.

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: What is the output of: string str = "Spider" + "man"; cout << str;
quizData.explanation: The strings are glued together. Since there is no space between them, the output is 'Spiderman'.
